Alt text: Yael sits in a sunlit studio, pulling an iPhone on a dolly toward her as she films her opposite hand. Mushrooms rest on her fingertips against a blue sky backdrop. The scene shifts to a bright green forest, where she forages mushrooms and moves through the greenery with her cane. Back in the studio, she sketches, slices the mushrooms, and carefully places them on her hand. In the final shot, her hand rises against the blue sky in a surreal scene, with mushrooms seemingly growing from each finger.

In this photo from our archives, photographer Chris Johns captured a culex mosquito crawling toward the eye of an 'i'iwi—a type of songbird also known as a honeycreeper. Many species of Hawaiian honeycreepers are considered critically endangered, not only due to climate change and habitat loss, but also because of the spread of avian malaria via mosquitoes.

See more Photos of the Day at the link in bio.

Photographed by National Geographic Explorer Ranita Roy (@ranita1roy), Kavita Mehra (right) and her neighbor Himanshu Singh Mehra (left) pick rhododendrons to make juice and jam. Mehra remembers when her home in Uttarakhand, India, was surrounded by forests full of the flowers, but the crimson-petaled trees are rarer now due in large part to illegal land clearing for development.

They’re perfectly designed to lie stock still for long periods of time—yet when it’s time to catch dinner, they’re fast as lightning. No wonder @joelsartore loves softshell turtles.

Premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD), a form of PMS that can trigger severe depression, intense anxiety, and other debilitating physical and psychological symptoms, affects around 3 to 8 percent of women of reproductive age. For decades, experts thought it was caused by hormonal imbalances, but a new body of research tells a more nuanced story—reshaping not just our understanding of the condition but how it can be treated.

Find out more about PMDD, its effects, and what researchers are learning about treating it at the link in bio.

Many scientists have dismissed acupuncture as little more than a placebo, but advanced imaging and clinical research reveal measurable changes in the body during treatment. As the evidence continues to grow, experts are rethinking where this ancient practice belongs in modern pain relief.

Learn about the possible benefits of acupuncture at the link in bio.

Leopard seals can open their jaws to remarkable angles, a frightening prospect for prey. “They have a reputation for being ferocious and a little terrifying,” says National Geographic Explorer @kiliiiyuyan, who encountered this seal off the Antarctic Peninsula. Sensing the seal was more playful than aggressive, Yüyan hung off the side of his inflatable boat, snapping pics as his playmate opened wide.

Photos by Keith Ladzinski @ladzinski | A nesting osprey carries a freshly caught trout toward its nest. Ospreys’ curved talons have reversible outer toes that help lock slippery prey firmly in place during flight. Once chicks hatch, a breeding pair may deliver dozens of fish to their nest each day, sometimes catching upward of seven fish an hour during peak feeding periods, as rapidly growing young demand a near constant supply of food. Found on every continent except Antarctica, ospreys are among the world’s most widely distributed birds of prey, thriving anywhere healthy waterways provide the fish populations their survival depends on.

A heartbreaking incident from Rajasthan’s Kota has shocked the nation after 10-year-old Mayank Meena was allegedly murd*red following a minor dispute over ₹100–200.

According to reports, the accused — said to be the victim’s 16-year-old friend — allegedly took Mayank away before attacking him with a stone. Police claim the accused repeatedly hit the child on the head and later dumped the body near the railway workshop area.

The body was reportedly discovered nearly 30 hours later in a horrific condition, sparking outrage and grief across social media. Viral CCTV footage allegedly shows the accused leading Mayank away shortly before the crime.

Kota Police have arrested the accused and further investigation is currently underway. 🚔

A major tragedy unfolded in Bihar after a boat capsized in the Ganga River near Umanath Ganga Ghat in Patna’s Barh subdivision. According to initial reports, several people are feared dead after the overcrowded vessel overturned in the middle of the river.

Authorities confirmed that two bodies have been recovered so far, while rescue teams continue searching for missing passengers. SDRF personnel, local police, and divers have been deployed at the site as the rescue operation remains underway.

Eyewitnesses said panic broke out moments after the boat lost balance and capsized due to strong river currents. Many passengers reportedly jumped into the water in an attempt to save themselves.

Officials are yet to confirm the exact number of people missing, while an investigation has been launched into the incident. The tragedy has triggered concern over river safety and overcrowded boats in the region.

A shocking incident from Janeshwar Mishra Park in Lucknow has sparked outrage after a viral video allegedly showed a private security guard dragging and assaulting a man in front of his wife and children.

The disturbing clip, widely shared on social media, reportedly captured a heated confrontation inside the park premises, where the man was seen being pulled across the ground as family members tried to intervene. The video triggered strong reactions online, with many users demanding strict action against those involved.

Aslan – Kova Eksende bir dolunay gerçekleşti... 

2. Ev Aslan 8. Ev Kova ve buna eşlik eden güçlü bir Stelyum

Bu eksen aslında şunu anlatır:
Bireysel güç (Aslan) - Sistem gücü (Kova)

Türkiye haritasında bunun anlamı
2 ev Aslan: “Bizim gücümüz, bizim değerimiz”
8. ev Kova: “Sistemlerin, ağların, küresel güçlerin kontrolü”

Ve dediğim gibi: 8. evde Kova stelyumu var:
Bu stelyuma eşlik eden gezegenler ise: Plüto• Mars• Güneş• Vesta

Bu çok sıradan bir görünüm değil.
Bu bir “sistem aktivasyonu”.

Kova’nın ezoterik anlamı ise şudur
Kova sadece özgürlük değildir.
Kadim anlamda Kova:Kolektif akıl 
Görünmeyen ağlar • Teknoloji • İdeoloji • Devrim
Ama aynı zamanda soğuk, mekanik, insanüstü sistemler

Ezoterik dilde Kova =“İnsan aklının Tanrı rolüne soyunmasıdır.”

Toparlayacak olursak 
8ev = Gizli güç, Kriz, Kontrol, Karma
Kova = Sistem, Ağ, Kolektif bilinç
Birleşince Gizli sistemler.

Türkiye haritasında bu:Küresel finans sistemi
• Uluslararası güç ağları • Görünmeyen anlaşmalar • Stratejik bağımlılıklar
• İdeolojik projeler temasını gösterir.

ŞİMDİ TEK TEK BU STELYUMA EŞLK EDEN GEZEGENLERİN NE İFADE ETTİĞİNİ AÇIKLAYALIM

☉ Güneş (merkez güçtür)
8. ev Kova’da ise Türkiye’nin kaderinde “sistemlerle yüzleşme” var der. 

♂ Mars (çatışmadır)
8. ev Kova’da ise : Sistemlerle çatışma kaçınılmaz der. 
Ama bu açık savaş değil; daha çok ekonomik, teknolojik ve ideolojik bir savaştır. 

♇ Plüto (derin güç) 
8. ev Kova’da ise: Gizli güçlerle dönüşüm der. 
Plüto burada çok kritik bir noktadadır. 
• Eski güç yapıları yıkılır • Yeni güç yapıları kurulur • Ama süreç sancılıdır

Vesta (adanmışlık)
8. ev Kova’da: Türkiye’nin kaderinde bir “sistem misyonu” var der
Vesta burada:Ülkenin belirli bir ideolojiye veya misyona adandığını  ama bunun bedelinin ağır olduğunu gösterir.

Şimdi geldik Aslan Dolunayın bu stelyumu nasıl tetiklediğine

Dolunay 2. ev Aslan’da.
Yani:“Benim gücüm” diyen Aslan “Sistem gücü” diyen Kova’ya karşı.
Bu bir astrolojik olay değil. 
Bu bir güç savaşı.

Kolektif düzeyde ise anlamı
Bu dolunayla birlikte:

1) Halk ve Sistem gerilimi artar 
Bireysel çıkar - Kolektif düzen
Halkın ihtiyaçları - sistemin gereklilikleri

Bazı gezegenler sadece etkiler… 

Plüton ise dönüştürür.

Bazen yıkar, bazen yüzleştirir, bazen de seni senden çıkarıp yeniden doğurur.

Bu yayında; gölge tarafı, takıntıları, güç savaşlarını, karmik dönüşümü ve Plüton’un gerçek etkilerini konuşacağız.

1-Kolektifin şu anki bilinç hali – Kılıç Prensi
Merak, araştırma, sorgulama.
Toplum “soru sorma” modunda. Özellikle bilgi, medya, teknoloji, yapay zekâ, iletişim alanlarında hızlı bir zihinsel hareket var.
Ama aynı zamanda aceleci ve provoke olmaya açık bir enerji.
Bilgi savaşı dönemi.

2-Bastırılmış gölge – Kılıç Kraliçesi
Soğukluk. Mesafe. Duygudan kopuş.
Toplum mantıklı görünmeye çalışıyor ama kalp kapanmış durumda. Empati eksikliği, keskin yargılar, “haklı olma” ihtiyacı.
Akıl kalbi bastırmış.

3-Kırılacak eski sistem – Değnek Prensi
Kontrolsüz heyecan.
Ani çıkışlar, plansız liderlikler, ateşli ama olgunlaşmamış idealler.
“Devrim” isteyen ama stratejisi olmayan yapıların sarsılması.
Saf cesaret değil, bilinçli hareket dönemi geliyor.

4-Ani şok alanı – Kılıç Sekiz
Kısıtlanma algısı.
“Özgür değiliz” hissinin zirve yapması. Sansür, baskı, psikolojik sıkışmışlık gündem olabilir.
Ama burada önemli şey şu:Bu kart gerçek hapisten çok zihinsel hapistir.
Kolektif, zincirin kendi zihninde olduğunu fark edecek.

5-Uyanan yeni bilinç – Tılsım Üç
Birlikte üretim.Topluluk bilinci. Kooperatif sistemler. Alternatif ekonomi.
Bireysel kahraman dönemi değil, kolektif inşa dönemi.
 “Ben” değil “biz”.

6-Ruhsal destek – Kılıç Dört
Durma. Geri çekilme.
Kolektife zorunlu bir dinlenme, yavaşlama enerjisi geliyor.
Belki küresel ölçekte bir “pause” hissi.
Bu kart ilahi koruma gibi çalışır:
“Şimdi sus ve izle.”

7-6 ay içindeki yön – Kupa Ası
Ve işte kalp açılıyor 💧
Yeni bir merhamet dalgası.
Sanat, spiritüellik, empati, kalpten bağlantılar.
Bu tutulma önce zihni sarsıyor, sonra kalbi açıyor.

ama her zaman kaçınılmazdır.

“Herkes böyle yaşıyor” kalıbının kırılması.

Bu kavuşumda:Gizli güç oyunları açığa çıkar& Psikolojik bağlar kopar&“Neden buradayım” sorusu yanar&Eski ruh sözleşmeleri bozulur

Antik metinlerde Mars–Plüton kavuşumları:
Gizli tarikatların çözülmesi
Yasak bilginin ortaya çıkışı
Toplumsal tabu kırılmaları
Bilim–okült çatışmaları

“Güç artık tek elde değil, bilinçte.”
Bu yüzden bu kavuşum:Diktatör değil,isyan değil sistemi içeriden çökerten bilinçtir.

Bu kavuşum bilinçaltında şunu tetikler:
Kontrol ihtiyacı,güçsüz kalma korkusu “ya her şey dağılırsa” paniği

Mars–Plüton:Bastırılmış öfkeyi,saklanan arzuyu,söylenmemiş “hayır”ları bilince çıkarır.
Bu yüzden:Ani kopuşlar,sessiz uzaklaşmalar
içten içe bitişler olur.

🃏 XX – MAHKEME (Judgement / Diriliş)
Ama klasik anlamıyla değil.

Mahkeme Kartı’nın gizli anlamı
Mahkeme kartında:Gökten inen bir ses (ilahi çağrı) mezardan çıkan insanlar eski bedenlerin terk edilmesi yeni bir bilinçle ayağa kalkış

Kadim ezoterik anlamı:“Ruhun, eski kimliğinden çağrılması.”

Bu kart:Ölümden sonra gelen diriliş değil, bilincin yeniden başlatılmasıdır.

🜂 Neden Kova Stelyumu = Mahkeme?

Çünkü:
Kova = Kolektif bilinç
8 ev = Ölüm / yeraltı / bilinçaltı
Plüton = Yeniden doğuş
Mars = Ayağa kalkış
Merkür = Çağrı
Güneş = Kimlik
Venüs = Değerler
Vesta = Kutsal ateş

Hepsi birleşince:
İnsanlık, eski benliğinden “çağrılıyor”.
Mahkeme kartı bu yüzden “gizli anahtar”dır.

🧨 Mahkeme kartının karanlık versiyonu 
Tarot’un hermetik geleneğinde Mahkeme kartının gölgesi vardır.

“Uyanışa direnmek.”

Bu dönemde bazı insanlar:Delirmiş gibi hisseder,gerçeklikten kopar,depresyona girer
eski kimliğe tutunur. 

Çünkü Mahkeme kartı sorar:“Hazır mısın?”

Hazır değilsen, süreç acı verir.

🌳 KABALA’DA BU SÜRECİN “YASAK ADI”

Kabala’da bazı süreçlerin adı açık söylenmez.
Çünkü isim, enerjiyi çağırır.

Bu sürecin adı:
⚫ “Şevirat ha-Kelim” (שבירת הכלים)

Türkçesi:Kapların Kırılması

Kapların Kırılması ne demek?

Kabala’ya göre:Tanrısal ışık dünyaya inerken
onu taşıyacak kaplar vardı ama kaplar ışığı taşıyamadı ve kırıldı ışık parçalandı dünya oluştu. 

Ezoterik anlamı:İnsan bilinci, hakikati taşıyamadığı için parçalandı.

Kova Stelyumu = Kapların yeniden kırılması
Bu dönem:Eski inanç kapları kırılıyor,eski kimlik kapları kırılıyor,eski sistem kapları kırılıyor,eski ilişkiler kapları kırılıyor

Çünkü yeni bilinç daha güçlü.

Bu yüzden insanlar:“Hiçbir şey eskisi gibi değil” diyor,güven duygusunu kaybediyor anlam arıyor,
mistisizme yöneliyor ya da tamamen maddeye kaçıyor. 

Hepsi Şevirat ha-Kelim’in belirtileri.

C. G. Jung 🔴 Kırmızı Kitap

Jungun Ruhuyla Konuşmaları

Jung: “Ruhum , sen misin? Nice yükseklerden ve uzaklardan konuşuyorsun?

Ruh: “Senden yukarıdayım . Ben ayrı bir dünyadayım . Güneş gibi oldum. Ateşin

Jung: Aşağıda, loş yeryüzündeyim . Ateşin bize bıraktığı karanlık dumandayım ve seni 
göremiyorum . Yine de sesin yakından geliyor.’

Run: “Hissediyorum . Yeryüzünün ağırlığı içime işliyor, ıslak soğuk kefen gibi sarıyor beni,

Jung: “ Yeryüzünün dumanlarına, karanlığına inme. Üzerinde çalış­maya devam ediyorum ve güneş gibi kalmanı isterim . Yoksa aşağıda, 
yeryüzünün karanlığında yaşama yürekliliğimi yitiririm . Sesini işiteyim yeter. Seni bir daha ete kemiğe bürünmüş görmek istemiyorum . Bir şey söyle! Derinliklerden, belki de korkunun içime aktığı yerden.’

Ruh:“Yapamam çünkü yaratıcı kaynağın oradan akıp geliyor.’

In the latest episode I’m joined by writer Courtney Ann LaFaive @kortnijajajajaja to discuss the life and work of the famous 20th century astrologer Linda Goodman, who wrote the highest-selling astrology book of all time.

In 1968, Linda’s book Sun Signs became a runaway phenomenon, selling millions of copies and becoming the first astrology book to make the New York Times bestseller list. Goodman’s approachable writing style appealed to a wide variety of readers, and helped to catalyze a massive boom in astrology in the late 1960s and 70s, serving as the gateway for millions of people to discover their full birth chart.

She then followed this up in 1978 with another successful book titled Love Signs, which was on relationship compatibility between zodiac signs. This book set a record at the time for the highest advance ever paid to an author, which was 2.25 million dollars, and helped to make the question “What’s your sign?” a ubiquitous cultural catchphrase.

However in her later years Goodman struggled to cope with immense personal loss, most notably the tragic death of her daughter in 1973, and her inability to distinguish between reality and illusion.

Courtney is the author of a new book titled Follow the Signs: Searching for Linda Goodman, America’s Forgotten Astrology Queen. The book is a memoir about a period of her life spent researching Linda Goodman’s biography for her PhD thesis, and the difficulties she faced in the process of piecing together Linda’s nebulous story.

Together, we explore and attempt to document the incredible life, complex legacy, and lasting astrological impact of Linda Goodman.

This is episode 536 of The Astrology Podcast, and you can watch or listen to it now wherever you listen to podcasts!

Astrologer Lee Lehman passed away yesterday. Lee was one of the pioneers in the early revival of traditional astrology, from the mid-1980s onward. She was particularly well-known for her work on horary astrology, which she helped to re-popularize in the United States. I first learned horary from Lee at Kepler College, where she was deeply involved as a dean and instructor in their degree program. That's why the main episode I did on horary, episode 116 of The Astrology Podcast, was with Lee. Her 2002 book The Martial Art of Horary Astrology is still a particularly brilliant work on Renaissance style horary. 

She had a long list of accolades in our community, such as winning a Regulus award, being the first horary astrologer to win the Marc Edmund Jones Award, and being involved in a leadership capacity in different organizations. Lee was also one of the few openly gay astrologers in a time when it was not yet as easy or accepted to be in the astrological community. Her work was characterized by her rigorous and academic approach to traditional astrology, and her unapologetic attitude towards following her heart and interests even when it set her apart helped to shape the astrological community into what it is today.
Linda Goodman's Sun Signs book was flying off the shelves when it first came out in 1968, and it spent an impressive six months on the New York Times bestsellers list.

The Mercury-Mars conjunction today is the fourth in a rare series of five conjunctions, a cluster that only forms every 15-17 years. The most notable recent example was in 2008/09 when the Global Financial Crisis hit, which was itself an exact 79-year repetition of when this same series coincided with the Wall Street Crash of 1929. It also happened in 1978/9 during the Iranian Revolution and subsequent oil crisis.

Uranus moves into Gemini today for the next 7 years. This happens every 84 years, and historically has coincided with the three most important wars in US history: the Revolutionary War, Civil War, and WWII.

Astrologers have long noticed that the transit of Uranus through Gemini often coincides with important turning points in United States history, and in episode 376 of the podcast Nick Dagan Best joined me to talk about what these transits coincided with in US history so that we can better anticipate what the next transit will coincide with when it begins in 2025.

In the lastest episode I trace the history and origins of the astrological technique called midpoints back to two students of Placidus who lived in the 17th century, and then tell the story of its recovery and popularization by German astrologers in the early 20th century.

For the past twenty years, the history of midpoints has been shrouded in mystery. Many astrologers have speculated about its origins, with myths tracing the technique back to the 13th-century astrologer Guido Bonatti, the 2nd-century astrologer Claudius Ptolemy, or assuming it was invented from scratch by Alfred Witte and the Hamburg School around the 1920s. After spending a while researching the history, I’m excited to present the conclusions I came to and help set the record straight.

It turns out that the midpoint technique actually originated in 1675. That is when two students of the famous astrologer Placidus, named Francesco Brunacci and Francesco Maria Onorati, introduced the concept of what they called “equidistances” in an appendix to the second edition of their teacher’s work, Physiomathematica.

However, this technique almost didn’t survive. Throughout the episode, I discuss how the midpoint doctrine went through two distinct periods of suppression: first by the Catholic Church and the Inquisition in the late 17th century, and later by the Nazis in Germany during the 1930s and 1940s.

We follow the lineage of the technique from its near-erasure in Italy, to its survival via a subtle reference by the 17th-century astrologer Antonio Francesco de Bonattis (not to be mistaken for Guido Bonatti), and finally to its rediscovery by Albert Kniepf in Germany at the beginning of the 20th century. From there, we explore how the technique was championed and popularized by Alfred Witte and the Hamburg School and Uranian Astrology, as well as by Reinhold Ebertin through his approach called Cosmobiology, eventually becoming a staple of late 20th-century astrology.

The mystery of the "golden orb" has finally been cracked!

Remember that strange, shiny gold blob scientists found two miles deep in the Gulf of Alaska back in 2023? After more than two years of scientific investigation, we finally have an answer.

Turns out, it’s not an extraterrestrial egg or a lost piece of pirate treasure... it’s the remnants of a giant deep-sea anemone (Relicanthus daphneae)!

Specifically, it was the base of the anemone that helps it stick to rocks. The anemone either died or decided to pack up and move house, leaving its shiny golden "foot" behind for us to find.

Science truly is the ultimate detective story. 

Check out the full reveal in this special #EarthDayNOAA feature story by clicking on the "golden orb" link in our bio.

The tidal level records at the Honolulu geophysical observatory were really on a roll in the 60s!

This bank of recording instruments recorded coastal water level observations at remote sites throughout Pacific Basin on large rolls of paper. They were part of the U.S. Tsunami Warning System, which NOAA and its predecessors have been running for more than 50 years.

Today NOAA has much more advanced recording and analysis tools to monitor for tsunamis and the earthquakes and other phenomena that cause them so that we can provide timely and accurate tsunami messages, but these old analog recorders did their job admirably in their time.

Catch up with the NOAAstalgia series at https://www.noaa.gov/heritage/noaastalgia-picturing-our-past.

(Credit: NOAA)

To kick off #EarthMonth we are bringing you stunning views taken by NOAA’s South Pole researchers as they said goodbye to the sun for the season! 👋

Did you know that South Pole station staff experience just 1 sunrise and 1 sunset per year?! 

"The sunset marks a shift for the station. Winter is here and with it the long, cold night. Last month, the final planes departed, leaving behind the 45 winterovers that will remain isolated for eight months. Spirits are high and there is much excitement for the dark night sky to arrive. We will miss the sun, but not too much." said NOAA technician Luke Longren. 

“Watching the sun trace the horizon over night and day, sinking lower as time passes, feels as if we’ve left our world for one far less familiar. It’s truly a privilege to experience something so few people get to witness." said NOAA station chief LT Mike Doneghey.

Learn more about @noaagml South Pole research at the link in our bio.

The Adhik Maas Purnima on 30th May 2026 is considered one of the most spiritually significant full moons of the year. Falling during the sacred Purushottam Maas, this day is believed to amplify prayers, charity, mantra chanting, and devotional practices.

In Hindu traditions, Purnima is associated with emotional balance, mental clarity, and divine blessings. When it occurs during Adhik Maas, devotees believe its spiritual benefits become even more powerful.

Many people spend this day worshipping Lord Vishnu, offering prayers to the Moon, performing charity, and seeking blessings for peace, prosperity, and family well-being.
Things you can do on this day:
• Offer Arghya to the Moon with devotion
• Light a ghee diya and pray for abundance
• Chant Vishnu mantras and read spiritual scriptures
• Practice gratitude, charity, and positive intentions

Remember, remedies are believed to work best when performed with faith, sincerity, and good deeds.

Is your brand actually profitable, or are you just outrunning a customer acquisition (CAC) cost monster?

Chapter 3 of The Ecommerce Playbook is live, and it’s a masterclass in unit economics taught by Mehtab Bhogal. 

Mehtab’s Golden Rules of ecommerce finance: 
• A high average order value (AOV) is your best friend. This gives you room to market your product and outlast macro shocks like supply chain issues. 
• Margin is your cushion. Aim for 15-20% net profit to survive the unexpected, like tariffs or shipping hikes. 
• Avoid cost-plus pricing. Your customers don't care what it costs you to make your product. They care about value. 
• A big inventory means frozen cash. Don't let your profits sit on a warehouse shelf.

Mehtab can help you master the numbers that decide whether you scale or stall. Read all of Chapter 3 by downloading The Ecommerce Playbook. Link in the bio!

• Above the fold matters, start A/B testing here. 100% of visitors see the top of your page first. 
• Images and words are (almost) the whole ballgame. Focus on resonant copywriting and quality visuals. 
• Make your checkout as boring as possible. Distractions destroy conversions, so strip your cart of everything except the buy button.

If the sky is pink and white… 🎵⁣
⁣
@NASAChandraXray and @NASAWebb came together to give us this stunningly pink image of Westerlund 2, a giant star cluster. The stars in this image are between one and three million years old.⁣
⁣
Data from the two telescopes has revealed not only newborn stars, but brown dwarfs – objects that are bigger than planets, but smaller than stars – and swirls of gas.⁣
⁣
#NASA #Stars #Space #Galaxy

I’m so hungry, I could eat two million Suns!

In this photo taken by @NASAChandraXray and @NASAWebb, a supermassive black hole at the center of a spiral galaxy is feasting on its surroundings. This black hole is about 60 million light-years from Earth and has a mass of two million Suns—and it’s only growing larger.

With supermassive black holes like this, scientists have wondered which comes first: the galaxy or the black hole? New data from Webb may help to explain how some black holes can grow so big without a larger galaxy to feed them. Check our link in bio for more details!

#NASA #blackhole #galaxy

An (inter)stellar side profile!
 
This photo of a spiral galaxy named UGC 10043 was snapped by @NASAHubble. It is located about 150 million light-years away from Earth in the constellation Serpens.
 
Spiral galaxies like our Milky Way are known for their swirling arms, so why does this image just show a straight line? It’s because we are viewing this galaxy on its edge rather than looking at its face.
 
This galaxy is mostly disc-like in shape, but a round glow of light protrudes from its center on both sides. That is its central bulge, a tight concentration of older stars — a structure that’s classic to spiral galaxies.
 
Credit: ESA/Hubble & NASA, R. Windhorst, W. Keel
 
#NASA #Space #Galaxy

Stop, you’re making me blush 😊

Nebulae are full of the ingredients needed to make new stars, and gravity is the catalyst that turns these materials into young, developing stars called protostars. 

The protostars in this nebula, photographed by @NASAHubble, are particularly large, throwing radiation and stellar wind out into the surrounding space. The young stars’ radiation excites the hydrogen gas in this nebula, making it emit light (depicted in pink).

This nebula, named RCW 7, is located 5,300 light-years from Earth, and can be spotted in the constellation Puppis.

#NASA #Hubble #Nebula #CottonCandy

A “starry” galaxy far, far away
 
Peek behind the bright star at the center of this image and you’ll see what appears to be a swarm of faint stars. This is actually a dwarf irregular galaxy residing some 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. (“Dwarf irregular” means this galaxy has an unusual shape. It’s not a spiral, round, oval, or lenticular – kind of a cross between a spiral and oval.)
 
This image comes from a @NASAHubble program that looked at the movement of galaxies and galaxy clusters through space. Matter in the universe is distributed unevenly, and the gravitational influence that matter has drives the movement of large-scale structures in the universe.
 
#NASA #Hubble #Space #Galaxy

A stellar (air) glow up ✨

In this image of Earth from the @ISS, Earth appears to be bathed in orange light. This is due to a phenomenon called airglow, where molecules in the upper atmosphere release energy after being excited by radiation from the Sun. This glow can be a variety of colors, including red, green, purple, and yellow.

Airglow is constantly shining throughout Earth’s atmosphere, but it is so dim that it can only be seen in orbit or by a sensitive camera from the ground with dark skies.

Credit: ESA/Sophie Adenot

#NASA #Astrophotography #Earth

Did you catch the Moon-Venus conjunction?

On the night of May 18, our Moon and Venus had a celestial meetup, called a conjunction. Their position in the sky made them appear close together, despite being millions of miles apart in space.

Credit: NASA/Bill Ingalls

#NASA #Moon #Venus #Conjunction

What does the Milky Way look like from space?

This photo was taken by NASA astronaut Chris Williams on April 13, 2026, from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft docked to the International Space Station. If you can find a dark enough sky, you may be able to see a similar view back on Earth!

Credit: NASA/Chris Williams

#NASA #Space #Stargazing

A view earned only by leaving the world that made us.

From low Earth orbit, the planet reveals itself in motion — aurora sweeping across the night side, the atmosphere glowing in thin, electric layers, and pieces of the International Space Station drifting quietly through view. In this timelapse, astronauts capture the rhythm of Earth as only orbit can show it: fast, fluid, and alive.

Every frame is a reminder of why we study our home planet from above — to understand its systems, protect its future, and witness the beauty that unfolds just beyond the horizon.

Demonstrating VIPER's path from lander to lunar surface 🌕

We completed a critical demonstration of our offloader mechanism, using rotating arms and cables to safely lower a VIPER mass simulator from the top of Blue Moon MK1's forward module to the lunar surface.

Melt. Extract. Fuel. Repeat. 🚀

To build an affordable, sustainable presence on the Moon, we must learn to live off the land. Air Pioneer is a scalable, modular system that creates purified oxygen to reduce dependency on supplies from Earth. Using Moon-extracted oxygen for propellant, fuel cells, and breathing reduces our lunar landers’ load by many metric tons of mass. Launching less mass from Earth lowers the cost of our Moon base, fueling a future cislunar economy. 🌕

Falcon 9 launches 24 Starlink satellites from California
Aurora watching into an orbital dawn. This never gets old!

Denver, Colorado as seen from the ISS. The Denver airport, one of the largest in the world, is the dark patch right of center. To the West, city lights are abruptly extinguished by the Rocky Mountains.

Behold the “space cake.” Making cakes on the @iss with our rather limited “baking” supplies is an art, requiring creativity and resourcefulness. I loved making cakes for my crewmates on my first mission and considered myself adept, but our cosmonaut colleagues on Expedition 74 have certainly taken it to the next level. Here’s an example of their recent work, in celebration of 100 days in space for @astro_hathaway and @soph_astro, and my 300th day. It tasted even better than it looked!

Another glimpse inside the @iss Life Sciences Glovebox (LSG). It was time to provide the neocartilage mentioned in last week’s post with some fresh nutrients, so yesterday I conducted a media exchange. This nifty chamber designed for space has septa on each side, so I can attach 2 syringes – one to withdraw the old media, while simultaneously injecting fresh media. The glovebox helps us prevent contamination in the chamber and also protects us and the space station atmosphere from any potentially harmful chemicals.

In this timelapse of the night sky, you can see lightning storms (beginning and end), and one of my favorite night scenes – as we cross over Africa from west to east, it is very dimly lit until the snaking spectacle of light along the Nile appears (worth the wait), erupting into the brilliance of Cairo and the river delta. It’s a perfect illustration of the vitality of our planet’s water sources and how our human cultures have developed along them.

[🇫🇷 version FR en commentaire] Day 105, orbit 1629 — This accelerated video shows the installation of MatISS-4, a French experiment that aims to collect and study the biocontamination of the air inside the European Columbus module. Biocontamination – such as small skin cells, droplets from sneezes or saliva, and bacteria 🦠 – is unavoidable, so we make sure to clean surfaces every week (I’ll publish a housekeeping timelapse soon!), but some areas are particularly hard to reach…
 
Over 10 years, MatISS has shown that the Station’s systems are very efficient; after several months of exposure, the contamination level inside the MatISS cases remains low. MatISS-4 features a new design, which will allow the contaminated membranes to be analysed at the @esrf_synchrotron in Grenoble using an X-ray nano-imaging instrument 🔬. Go science! 

MatISS has also enabled researchers to identify surfaces that are more resistant to biocontamination, opening up many applications on Earth: in public transportation, where thousands of people touch the same surfaces every day, in places where limiting contamination is key, such as hospitals, cruise ships, and submarines, and even in livestock farming. Less biocontamination = less cleaning with chemical products!   

🎥 @europeanspaceagency / @nasa 

#εpsilon • @iss • @nasajohnson • @cnes_france

Growing stem cells in space to help treat cancer and disease. 🚀

People who need to build up their blood system to fight disease or recover from chemotherapy can benefit from receiving blood stem cells. Researchers are working to produce high-quality stem cells in larger quantities in space. Results could help improve patient outcomes for people with fatal blood disorders, various blood cancers, and immune diseases. 🩸

Stem cells produced on Earth can lose their ability to form different types of cells like red and white blood cells or platelets. For therapies, it is critical that these cells can continue to divide over a long period of time. Space-grown cells may retain the ability to continuously divide over a longer duration, creating larger cell supplies for patients in need.

The space station continues to serve as a testbed for new technologies and medicine that have the potential to greatly benefit life on Earth. 🌍

Two cosmonauts went on a spacewalk on May 27 exiting the space station to install a solar radiation experiment that will measure bursts of solar radiation from solar flares. The duo also removed research gear that exposed of samples of semiconductor materials and microbes to the external space environment.

Pic 1) Roscosmos cosmonaut and Expedition 74 commander Sergey Kud-Sverchkov works outside the International Space Station in his Orlan spacesuit with red stripes during a six-hour and five-minute spacewalk.

Pic 2) Roscosmos cosmonaut and Expedition 74 flight engineer Sergei Mikaev works outside the International Space Station's Poisk module in his Orlan spacesuit with blue stripes during a six-hour and five-minute spacewalk.

Credit: NASA

[🇫🇷 version FR 👇] Day 106, orbit 1644 — This timelapse shows the approach and docking of the @spacex CRS‑34 🛰 resupply mission on 17 May. Isn’t it amazing to think that this docking manoeuvre takes place while flying at 28,000 km/h around the Earth?

The blurred dot in the middle of the picture is actually a micrometeoroid impact on the Cupola window… No worries, it’s been there for a long time !

🎥 @europeanspaceagency / @nasa 

Jour 106, orbite 1644 – Ce timelapse montre les phases d'approche et d’amarrage de la mission de ravitaillement @spacex CRS‑34 🛰 le 17 mai. Incroyable de se dire que cette manœuvre d’amarrage s’effectue à 28 000 km/h autour de la Terre !

Le point flou au centre de l’image est un impact de micrométéoroïde sur le hublot de la Cupola… mais pas d’inquiétude, il n’est pas récent !

#εpsilon • @iss • @nasajohnson

Expedition 74, how was your @SpaceX Dragon delivery? This time-lapse video shows Dragon approaching the International Space Station above the Indian Ocean, crossing the Himalayas into China, and then docking over the Pacific Ocean as the sun sets on May 17, 2026.

I’ve spent much of the last 3 days since the @spacex cargo vehicle arrived in another of my happy places on the @iss, the “LSG” or Life Sciences Glovebox, doing cell biology experiments. Extremely rewarding for a biologist turned astronaut like me! 

On Monday I thawed frozen platelets and bone-marrow megakaryocytes (cells that are integral to the formation of blood clots and the immune response), supplied them with fresh media (the nutrients they need to live and grow), and injected them into a biocell habitat (photo 1). 

Tuesday I exchanged the media for another experiment, this time to feed neocartilage (engineered cartilage tissue grown in a lab for surgical implantation) (photo 2). Some of these samples include tension mechanisms, which have shown to improve neocartilage properties. Interestingly, the mechanism of pure tension cannot be studied on Earth because of the constant presence of gravity.

Today (Wednesday), I exchanged the media for the cells in Monday’s experiment. @astro_hathaway assisted by shepherding cells and supplies from their incubators to the glovebox (photos 3 and 4). 

Fingers crossed that all of the cells are happy and thriving and can thus provide a better understanding of inflammatory and clotting conditions in both microgravity and back on Earth!

[🇫🇷 version FR 👇] Day 096, orbit 1489 — So happy and honoured to have been monitoring the @spacex CRS-34 cargo Dragon approach and docking on Sunday, 17 May, together with @astro_hathaway! It always feels a bit surreal to see another spacecraft catching up with us, flying in formation with the Station for a while and then slowly closing the distance… at the orbital speed of 28000km/h!

Next: about 3 tonnes of equipment to unload, including a lot of time critical science, and also supplies & food…

📸 @nasa / @europeanspaceagency – S. Adenot 

Jour 096, orbite 1489 – Très heureuse et honorée d’avoir surveillé l’approche et l’amarrage du cargo Dragon CRS-34 de @spacex ce dimanche 17 mai, avec @astro_hathaway ! Cela paraît toujours presque irréel de voir un autre engin spatial nous rejoindre, voler en formation avec la Station, puis se rapprocher tout en douceur… à la vitesse orbitale de 28000 km/h !

Au programme : près de 3 tonnes à décharger, dont de nombreuses expériences scientifiques qui ne peuvent pas attendre, des provisions et des fournitures…

#εpsilon • @iss • @nasajohnson

NASA’s SpaceX Crew13 astronauts – Jessica Watkins, Luke Delaney, and Joshua Kutryk – recently visited Kennedy Space Center, getting a hands-on look at how today’s work supports tomorrow’s exploration. 🌱🚀 

Inside the Space Systems Processing Facility, the crew and NASA astronaut Stan Love sampled fresh microgreens, part of ongoing research into fast-growing, nutrient-rich crops that could help sustain astronauts during long-duration missions. These small plants pack a big punch, and learning to grow them efficiently is key as crews start traveling farther from Earth. 

The astronauts also toured the Vehicle Assembly Building, where they viewed major Artemis III and Artemis IV hardware – another reminder that today’s Commercial Crew missions help prepare humanity for our return to the Moon and future deep space journeys. 

Photo credit: NASA/Kim Shiflett

[🇫🇷 version FR 👇] Day 101, orbit 1567 — 100 days in space already…Living and working aboard the International Space Station is becoming second nature, but each morning, as I open the Cupola shutters, I’m reminded of how extraordinary it really is. 

Over these past weeks, I’ve been constantly challenged, amazed, and inspired – by the work, by the views, and by the incredible teams on the ground who make all of this possible.

Looking forward to the next 100 days!

📸 @europeanspaceagency / @nasa – S. Adenot 

Jour 101, orbite 1567 – Déjà 100 jours dans l’espace… Vivre et travailler à bord de la Station spatiale internationale est presque devenu la routine. Et pourtant, chaque matin, en ouvrant les volets de la Cupola, la vue me rappelle à quel point cette aventure est extraordinaire.

Ces dernières semaines m’ont challengée, émerveillée et inspirée à travers le travail, les vues à couper le souffle et les équipes formidables au sol qui rendent tout cela possible.

Hâte de découvrir ce que les 100 prochains jours me réservent ! 

#εpsilon • @iss • @nasajohnson • @cnes_france

This photo illustrates three phenomena that surprised me when I arrived on board the @iss. I didn’t realize before seeing it with my own eyes that the Earth’s atmosphere would be so visible in the night sky, seemingly even more obvious at nighttime than daytime, nor that it would be so colorful! These orange and green colors on the horizon are airglow, a faint emission of light caused by chemical reactions and interactions between UV radiation and gases in our atmosphere (unlike aurora, which is caused by solar wind particles interacting with Earth’s magnetic field, airglow is present everywhere, all of the time). I was also surprised to see how blue and well lit the Earth can be at night, when a bright Moon illuminates it (Moonglow!). This photo was taken on May 30, the day prior to the full Moon. I hope that this provides a bit more @nasaartemis Moon Joy for all of us!

ये 3 गलतियाँ आपको कर सकती हैं कंगाल! 

क्या आप भी अनजाने में रात के समय ये काम कर रहे हैं? ज्योतिष शास्त्र और वास्तु के अनुसार, रात में कुछ काम करने से घर में नकारात्मक ऊर्जा (negative energy) आती है और माँ लक्ष्मी नाराज़ हो जाती हैं, जिससे गरीबी और कंगाली का सामना करना पड़ सकता है। 

भूलकर भी रात में न करें ये 3 काम:
1️⃣ रात में झाड़ू लगाना: ऐसा करने से घर की बरकत चली जाती है।
